entry.title: Customer appearing in UDM_SUPERVISOR but not appearing in UDM_SPECIALIST
entry.content:
Hi,

There is one customer appearing in Supervisor worklist UDM_SUPERVISOR, but not appearing in worklist of specialist UDM_SPECIALIST, although specialist already assigned in UDM_BP. I executed transaction UDM_GENWL, but issue still exists.

Please support to find out the root cause.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: ATP check for sto purchase orders
entry.content: In the mentioned SSCUI you can find the so-called checking rule (e. g. 'A'). This means that the Checking Group and Checking Rule together define the Scope of Check like in your screenshot. In your screenshot next to 'A' the label is 'Sales Order'. However, when using 'A' in STO then one can state that STO and Sales Order behave the same from Scope of Check perspective. There is no dedicated Checking Rule for STO such that the label would state 'STO'. Maybe this is the confusion. If not, please feel free to come back 🙂

entry.title: Re: Goods issue with wrong unit cost.
entry.content: Hi @jennie258fitz, Please refer to my following query. I appreciate your prompt response.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hi all! I'm an SAP developer for a lot of years now, and I think it's only time that I got more involved in this community. I've been using content from the SAP developers' community since the old SDN, and I've been helped quite a lot in my proffessional challenges. Now I'm planning to share a few ideas of my own, and maybe start interesting discussions.

Looking forward to it!

entry.title: Re: crystal report footer blank space at end
entry.content: Hi Don, it desktop viewer and My page setup is A4 in crystal report following is the page setup of crystal report and printer preference.

entry.title: Re: Goods issue with wrong unit cost.
entry.content: Hi @jennie258fitz, Thank you for the prompt response. The only thing I am concerned about is the value of the inventory. As the period end was approaching, the inventory value was corrected using the inventory counting method. Before inventory counting, the unit cost of all the items were corrected as well. However, the impacts concerned to GI and GR now needs to be corrected. I apologize, but will the reversal entries affect my inventory values or not, as the period end closure activity was already concluded.

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP: req.error inside bound action not highlighting target in Fiori Elements Object Page
entry.content:
Finally I came to conclusion that you cannot highlight errors when youre not in edit mode.
So I have to create this workaround:

const isActiveEntity = req.params[0].IsActiveEntity;

if (mandatoryFields.length) {
            if (!isActiveEntity) {
                for (const field of mandatoryFields) {
                    if (!item[field.mandatoryField]) {
                        req.error({
                            message: `The field ${field.description} is mandatory`,
                            target: `in/${field.mandatoryField}`,
                            code: 400,
                        });
                    }
                };
            } else {
                let mandatoryErrors = "";
                for (const field of mandatoryFields) {
                    if (!item[field.mandatoryField]) {
                        mandatoryErrors += `The field ${field.description} is mandatory` + "\n";
                    };
                };
                req.error({
                    message: mandatoryErrors,
                    code: 400
                });
            }
        };

 This works perfectly but cant highlight the targets unfortunately.

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model (SAP PaPM Cloud UM) Posting Strategy via SOAP Services
entry.content:
Did you know that you can perform a posting of document to your S/4 HANA system via SAP PaPM Cloud UM without the use of Remote Function Adapter (RFA)?

Yes, you read it right, even without the Remote Function Adapter, we can achieve posting of document by using the related SOAP Services of the BAPIs that are being used for each RFA type from SAP PaPM Cloud SM and use this SOAP Services to the Connection and in our Writer function.

For your reference about BAPIs, see documentation Remote Function Adapter.

I know you are already excited to see on how it is being done in SAP PaPM Cloud UM. So, let’s start!

Prerequisite:

  1. Cloud Connector Setup with HTTP/ HTTPS protocol with S/4 HANA system. For more information, please refer to Cloud Connector. In addition, you may also refer to blog post: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard and Universal Model Integration with SAP BW via OData Service directing to Creation of Connection in Cloud Connector part.
  2. UUID Field on an existing environment that will be used as Default key field in Connection.

Finance General Ledger & General Ledger Items

General Ledger makes use of the BAPI BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST. Since BAPIs are not directly usable in SAP PaPM Cloud UM, we will use the SOAP service JournalEntryBulkLedgerCreation in our created Connection. This SOAP service can perform a general ledger posting to your SAP S/4 HANA System and can perform multiple accounting documents in one run.

See documentation: Journal Entry by Ledger - Post.

If you would like to verify if this SOAP service is already implemented in your S/4 HANA System, follow the step-by-step procedure below:

  1. Go to the transaction code: SOAMANAGER

  2.  Search the SOAP Web Service if available.

    1. Go to Web Service Configuration
    2. Search for the SOAP Service, example: JournalEntryBulkLedgerCreation.
    3. Choose the existing service.

      If this service is not yet implemented, please contact the administrator of your S/4 HANA System and see reference from: How to setup the Web Service Configuration of Journal Entry API for S/4 HANA On-Premise System

SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model – Modeling for Finance General Ledger

1.       Creation of Connection in SAP PaPM Cloud UM with the details of Destination created from the pre-requisite.

  1. Add Connection ID
  2. Add Connection Description
  3. Choose the Destination name
  4. Press Help button on the Service Path
  5. Add the created UUIDME field
  6. Searched for the SOAP Service for General Ledger: JournalEntryBulkLedgerCreation
  7. Choose the Service
    Save the changes and all models will populate in the Connections screen.

2.       Choosing of Models that will be used in Modeling.

Multiple Models will be available once the Connection is created. For the General Ledger scenario, we will be needing only three Models which are:

  • JournalEntryCreateRequestBul1 – this Model will serve as a header of the payload for the SOAP service posting.
  • JournalEntryCreateRequest – this Model will serve as the body that contains the General Ledger information like Ledger Group, Company Code, etc.
  • Item – this Model contains the information the line Item for the General Ledger posting. Like debit / credit code, transaction value and currency.

     

    2.1 Choose the First Model which is which is JournalEntryCreateRequestBul1 and map the required fields like the image below:

    See documentation on how to add fields: SOAP Service.

    2.2 Since we only need specific Models from our connection, users also need to remove the sync relation and only keep the Second Model synced which is JournalEntryCreateRequest.

    2.3 Moving on to the Second Model, JournalEntryCreateRequest. Here are the fields that is currently mapped on my General Ledger scenario.

    UUIDMEJournalEntry.OriginalReferenceDocumentLogicalSystem
    MessageHeader.ID.$valueJournalEntry.LedgerGroup
    MessageHeader.CreationDateTimeJournalEntry.DocumentReferenceID
    JournalEntryCreateRequestBulkMessageLedger_KeyJournalEntry.DocumentHeaderText
    JournalEntry.OriginalReferenceDocumentJournalEntry.DocumentDate
    JournalEntry.OriginalReferenceDocumentTypeJournalEntry.CreatedByUser
    JournalEntry.AccountingDocumentTypeJournalEntry.CompanyCode
    JournalEntry.PostingDateJournalEntry.BusinessTransactionType

    The fields above are added in my Model for General Ledger posting via SOAP Service. You may only add the mandatory fields based on the documentation: Journal Entry by Ledger - Post.

    We have two (2) keys for this Model as the (1) UUIDME field will serve as primary key of its Model, while (2) JournalEntryCreateRequestBulkMessageLedger_Key is for association field Joining rows from its Parent Model (JournalEntryCreateRequestBul1).  

    Later, I will show you how we are going to use this association field in the Join condition of this Model Entity relations.

    2.4 This second Model JournalEntryCreateRequest also has other Model Relations synced. With our current General Ledger scenario, we need to remove the others and only keep the Sync relation on model Item.

    2.5 Last Model that we will be mapping is the Item, Here are the fields that are currently mapped on our General Ledger scenario.

    UUIDMEAssignmentReference
    JournalEntryCreateRequest_KeyAmountInTransactionCurrency.$value
    GLAccount.$valueAmountInTransactionCurrency.attributes.currencyCode
    DocumentItemTextAccountAssignment.CostCenter
    DebitCreditCode 

    On this model, we also have two (2) key fields which are (1) UUIDME, serving as the primary key of this model, while (2) JournalEntryCreateRequestBulkMessageLedger.JournalEntryCreateRequest_Key is for association field joining both rows from its parent model: JournalEntryCreateRequest.

    2.6 Since all fields are created and mapped, we can now configure the Join Condition from the first Model JournalEntryCreateRequestBul1. Follow the steps below:

    1. In the created Connection, choose the Model JournalEntryCreateRequestBul1 , by clicking its row.
    2. Scroll down and choose the Model Relation JournalEntryCreateRequest.
    3. Edit the Join condition accordingly to the format below:
      Join Condition: (Model Name + ‘.’ + Association Field) = UUIDME

      This step is important as it in SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model, association fields should be mapped properly to make a successful relation between two (2) models.

2.7 Perform the same steps for second Model JournalEntryCreateRequest. But this time, we are going to use the association field of the Model Item.

a. In the created Connection, choose the Model JournalEntryCreateRequest,
by clicking its row.
b. Scroll down and choose the Model Relation Item.
c. Edit the Join condition accordingly to the format below:
    Join Condition: (Model Name + ‘.’ + Association Field) = UUIDME

2.8 You may now save the changes by following the steps below:
      a. Choose Save
      b. Then Sync Models

Once all are synced and saved.  Synced Model Entities will now appear on our Manage Functions screen in the environment together with the Fields in the Manage Fields screen.

 

3.       Creation of Local Model Entities equivalent structure of Model Entities from Connection

Since we already have Model Entities where the Writer function can write. Now, we need to have a Local Model entity that has the same structure from the synced Models.

It is also advisable to create a Local field identical to the Added fields from connection. This is to avoid confusion when it comes to mapping.

On this scenario, I have added the prefix “UM” before the name of the original field from connection to have an indicator that it is coming from my Local Model Entities. See image below for example:

3.1 Create the first Local Model Entity with identical fields from Synced Model JournalEntryCreateRequestBul1.

An association field is created as we are going to have the same structure of Model Entities from Synced Models.

3.2 Create the second Local Model Entity with identical fields from Synced Model JournalEntryCreateRequest. 

Related Model entities are only allowed to have one editable data model. Therefore, the restriction of this second local Model entity is ‘Disable Odata draft’.

For more information, see Documentation: Model Entity .
Note: All fields are not just being shown but number and fields should be identical

3.3 Create the third Local Model Entity with identical fields from Synced Model Item.

Similar from step 3.2,  ‘Disable Odata draft’ configuration is relevant for this Model Entity as well.

For more information, see Documentation: Model Entity .

Since the association fields are added for both second and third model, we may now create Relations between the Parents and its related Model.

3.4 Add Relations to the first Parent Local Model Entity by following the steps below:

a. Choose Edit
b. Then press Create.
c. Add Relation ID
d. Add Description of the Relation
e. Choose type Composition of Many’.
f. Choose the second local model MEA02.
g. Save

 3.5 Add Relations to the second Parent Local Model Entity by following the steps below:

a. Choose Edit
b. Then press Create.
c. Add Relation ID
d. Add Description of the Relation
e. Choose type Composition of Many’.
f. Choose the third local model MEA03.
g. Save

3.6 Add the data records to the main Local Model Entity. In case you need to post multiple items in one document, you need to add more data rows in the MEA03. Similar with the image below:

If you may notice, we have two (2) rows in the MEA03 as it contains the Amount transaction components. When putting an Amount Transaction, it is a MUST to be balanced, that is why we have Positive and Negative values in the data records.

With the same row, you will also see that the values for DebitCreditCode are S and H it means:

S –Debit
H- Credit
Refer to the: Documentation from help Portal

Note: These three (3) Local Model Entities can be a single Model Entity / Flat Model Entity. But the data records MUST be aligned with Association field as our Models uses Related Models structure.

Even so, for this example, we just have used the three (3) Local Model Entities to avoid confusion in terms of mapping.

4.       Create a Model View on top of the three (3) Local Model Entities

With the current UI Implementation In Universal Model, users must create a Model View on top of the Local Model Entity that has a relation from other Local Model Entities. This is because we will not be able to see the other fields as it will still be needed to be implemented.

Therefore, I have created the Model View as an example that contains ALL the fields, we have from three (3) Local Model Entities.

Aside from containing all the fields, I have created an extra UUID field to make it as an alias to our association fields from Parent Local Model Entities, this is to avoid confusion for mapping later in the Writer function.

5.       Creation of the Writer function

With all Input and Outputs already created in our Environment. Users may now proceed with the creation of Writer.

5.1 Create the Writer function

Type: Delete and Insert – for an example, we are using Delete and Insert as the Writer is considering the Primary key being used for Writing. (So, if a user executed a writer that uses only Insert type, it might probably cause an error due to primary keys).
Connection – connection that the user have used from previous step.
Input – the Model View created from previous step(Contains all fields required)
Result Model Function – user must choose the Mother Model Entity which is JournalEntryCreateRequestBul1 as it will serve the first Model Entity to be written on following the next Models setup in the Model Relations

5.2 All identical fields should be mapped to each other to ensure that the specific data records will be written to the correct field. See image below for example:

5.3 More importantly, primary key fields (UUIDME) and Association fields from our Local Model Entity should be mapped to the same field from our Synced Model Entities (UUIDME) and secondary keys.

MEA01.UUIDME = JournalEntryCreateRequestBul1.UUIDME
MEA02.UUIDME = JournalEntryCreateRequest
MEA02.MEA01ASSOC = JournalEntryCreateRequest.JECR.Key
MEA03.UUIDME = Item
MEA03.MEA02ASSOC = JournalEntryCreateRequest.Item.Key

6.       Activation and Execution of the Writer function

6.1 Activate the Environment in SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model to use the latest
changes performed from previous steps.
6.2 Execute now the Writer function.

a. Choose the Writer function by pressing the checkbox.
b. Press Run
c. Notification will show that the Run is finished for the Writer function.

6.3 Check the logs of the Writer function and get the Message ID from Localize message.

a. Choose the Writer function by clicking its row (not checkbox)
b. Choose direct link of Event logs.
c. Select the runFunction event.
d. Copy the message ID.

 

7.       Search the Message ID in the S/4 HANA system

7.1 Go to the S/4 HANA Syste, search the Tcode SRT_MONI

SRT_MONI – is like an application log for web services that are being requested or accepted to the S/4 HANA system. Since we have sent a SOAP Service to the S/4 HANA system, it will be listed under the SRT_MONI at SOAP service is also a web service.

7.2 Paste the Message ID then press Execute:

7.3 Choose the generated logs and copy all available details that we can use for searching in ACDOCA Table.

7.4 Search for the posted document from the details of the message ID in previous step.

a. Go to SE16
b. Search for ACDOCA table.
c. Add the copied values from previous steps in Message ID.
d. Execute

From here, we can get the Document number, Company Code and Fiscal year that are posted in the ACDOCA table for specific searching of this document in FB03.

7.5 Display the document to verify if it is successfully posted.

a. Go to transaction code FB03.
b. Add the Document number, Company Code and Fiscal year from previous step.
c. Continue
d. See the document posted.

With this example, we achieved the General Ledger posting from SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model using SOAP Service: JournalEntryBulkLedgerCreation .

We are still preparing how to achieve the posting for these RFA Adapters below using the SOAP Services:

  • Accounts Payable
  • Accounts Receivable
  • Sales and Distribution
  • Purchase Order
  • Post Journal Entries for Group Reporting

Once these are prepared, I will update this blog post with additional SOAP Services that you can use for other Remote Function Adapter scenarios.

Hopefully to see you again, in next integration blog post!

entry.title: Creating a course in multiple languages
entry.content:
Hello,

What is the best way to create courses in SuccessFactors Learning for multiple language needs?

Specifically if there was 2 content objects per course… i.e. 2 for English, 2 for Spanish.

I understand there are curriculum requirements, but I feel there has to be a better way?

It would be able to select “mark as complete” when all objects within a folder in an item are completed. Unless this is an option and I’ve missed something?

thank you!

entry.title: SAP B1 SQL Dashboard User name and password not working
entry.content:
Dear all,

When I am trying to login to generate the Dashboard SAP Business SQL then it is not working.

It's showing error like Incorrect Username and Password.

SAP Business One SQL Version,  Dashboard 

Regards,

Nikunj

entry.title: Re: Concurrent Employment / Rehire with new employment: Reuse Data Records from Custom MDF Objects
entry.content: Hi, I'm not sure whether there is any workaround for this so I have raised an influence request, please vote on this so that we can reach 10 votes and get this noticed by SAP - https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/329397. Many thanks

entry.title: Re: Vendor Return process
entry.content:
We have been using Returns to Vendor (SD) Delivery process since some time and have also implemented some checks (enhancement) in the process...

The process indeed helps Business track the Returns to Vendor for a particular Purchase Order (MIGO).

entry.title: Re: PAPM On Premise - Unable to see "Calculation" tabs in modeling
entry.content:
Hi Daniela,
since on your colleague laptop everything is working fine, I would say this is due to your laptop configuration and not related to PaPM.
Please get in contact with your IT department - they should be able to solve your issue.
Thanks and KR,
Emanuele

entry.title: Re: Functional consultant creating CAP application using SAP Joule
entry.content: Interesting 👍

entry.title: Re: How to continue executing batch job even after error
entry.content:
Hello Sandra,

Thanks for the response, I am creating a delivery using batch job for program RVV50R10C and inside this we have implemented some validation at IF_EX_LE_SHP_DELIVERY_PROC~DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK and raising an error using message statement (TYPE E) for manual process (VL01N, VL02N) and we need to implement the same error for the batch job (RVV50R10C) without terminating the job.

As per your suggestion i am going to create a new custom function module with exception error_message to handle validation inside IF_EX_LE_SHP_DELIVERY_PROC~DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK using message statement.

Am i following the correct procedure, please guide me.

entry.title: Re: How to continue executing batch job even after error
entry.content:
Hello Turkaj,

It is a custom error message handled at IF_EX_LE_SHP_DELIVERY_PROC~DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK, but the execution program is RVV50R10C. Please help me how to raise error_message for my issue.

Creating a custom function module with exception error_message and write error message statement inside that function module at IF_EX_LE_SHP_DELIVERY_PROC~DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK will handle the issue?

entry.title: Re: crystal report footer blank space at end
entry.content: Hi Don, forgot to mention if I remove the report footer and print it, that will print only one page but whenever I add the report footer this is happening.

entry.title: ALMathon 2024 for SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
ALMathon focuses on SAP Partners who want to leverage SAP Cloud ALM and create extensions which are making tthe usage of SAP Cloud ALM more efficient as a partner of SAP.

The CHALLENGE 2024 was given as follows:

"Realize (!)  a Partner use case in SAP Cloud ALM.

Showcase in the system your partner scenario."

The challenge started in May, and it ends at SAP ALM Summit EMEA with the pitches of the 6 elected TOP teams.
The winning team will be decided by voting of all summit participants plus a jury of executive experts of SAP Cloud ALM. 
Stay tuned I will inform here in that blog, who the winner will be.

The ALM-athon is a virtual challenge of many partner teams who are actively contributing in the SAP Cloud ALM community. This year 12 teams have registered. The TOP 6 team pitches will be presented live on stage at the ALM Summit in Mannheim end of September.


Here you can find a detailed description and videos of the solutions from the teams who participated in the challenge.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NTT Data

The “ALM Navigator” offers a transformative approach to information integration and access by:

  • Centralized Data Hub: Unifying multiple data sources into one cohesive platform.
  • Comprehensive Integration: Merging diverse sources like SAP Cloud ALM, LeanIX, NTT DATA services, and SAP for Me into a single access point for various types of information.
  • AI-Orchestrated User Requests: Leveraging an AI ChatBot to deliver tailored information based on user roles within SAP Cloud ALM.
  • Role-Based Information Grouping: Delivering relevant data in a meaningful, role-specific order to enhance efficiency and decision-making.

This innovative combination of data sources and AI-driven personalization dramatically enhances how users interact with critical information, optimizing both workflow and productivity

Video

 

Swisscom

Solution Description: Customer business processes are created and optimized using SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, based on  usage data and customizing settings. From Signavio Process Manager these processes are transferred to SAP Cloud ALM by our Swisscom solution, the Business Process Model Genie.

Technologies Used: SAP BTP (ABAP RAP), SAP Signavio API, SAP Cloud ALM API

Problem Addressed: Missing link between SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM

Innovative Aspect: Intelligent, assisted generation of custom business processes based on real usage data and settings

Video

 

RidonIT

Solution “ALM Buddy”

Our "ALM Buddy" solution is an extension to SAP Cloud ALM that closes a series of current functional gaps and thus facilitates the transition to SAP Cloud ALM. By providing easily customizable functionalities for copy control, consistency checks, mail notifications and status-dependent mandatory field checks – to name but a few – the application significantly increases usability of SAP Cloud ALM. Overall, our solution allows for a maximum level of flexibility and at the same time ensures consistency over all processes and use cases within SAP Cloud ALM.

 

Video

 

 

      
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 


TwinDocs

Twin Docs is the ideal solution for long-standing SAP Solution Manager customers who have built up an extensive and complex solution documentation and plan a step-wise migration from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM. Our solution allows for both a repeatable uni-directional content transfer (with delta upload) and a continuous bi-directional synchronization of content between SAP Solution Manager 7.2 and SAP Cloud ALM.

TwinDocs facilitates the transition to SAP Cloud ALM by enabling customers to continuously transfer structures and content from their existing solution documentation to SAP Cloud ALM and set up a federated environment for the simultaneous operation of SAP Solution Manager and SAP Cloud ALM for solution documentation. This enables customers to safeguard their solution documentation investment without the need to implement new tools

Video

 

Software AG

Solution: Content with custom content

By Software AG’s team Consider MY process!

Processes play an important role in the implementation phase in SAP Cloud ALM. In many situations, custom content like process documentation is already available at customer side. Business Process Management (BPM) tools like ARIS are used to gather and maintain process information and to communicate processes throughout all relevant stakeholders.

These custom processes are relevant input (e.g., for Fit-2-Standard Workshops) for the SAP Cloud Implementation team. ARIS offers a new standard exchange capability to import BPMN models as well as SVG graphics for processes modeled in other notations. For BPMN based models imported from ARIS, tasks, pool, events, etc. are imported as model elements. This content (Solution Value Flow and assigned Solution Process Flows) is made available in Process Authoring App as well as in Processes App to work further on and assign Requirements, Notes etc.

Furthermore, SAP Cloud Implementation Team Member can easily navigate to the original ARIS model using accelerator links and access additional information, e.g., to better understand why the process is designed in the given way or which additional aspects are relevant.

 

Blueworks

 

TARDIS - Toolbox for Archiving and Recovery of Data in SAP Cloud ALM

This application enables the scheduling of snapshots for SAP Cloud ALM data, storing them in a self-hosted database (either in the cloud or on-premise, depending on your infrastructure). These snapshots allow for the recovery of data, whether it’s the entire dataset or selected portions. You can also compare live data in SAP Cloud ALM with different versions stored in the database, enabling the restoration of the desired version back into the system. This includes recovering data that has been deleted from SAP Cloud ALM. Additionally, the application supports importing data from other platforms like Jira, ServiceNow, and more, integrating it into SAP Cloud ALM.

Video

 

Basis Cloud Solutions

 

We are elated to announce our entry into ALMathon ALM Summit EMEA 2024 following our win at ALMathon at SAP ALM Summit at Bengaluru 2024 demonstrating auto-healing of SSL certificates and presenting the capabilities of our AI-powered co-pilot, Maestro, integrated with our Unified Cloud Orchestration Platform, Symphony.

Maestro is Symphony’s AI based co-pilot with an intuitive interface that can perform a wide range of activities across the application or technology portfolio, revolutionizing task execution and management through easy natural-language-based real time conversation.

  • Orchestrate: Maestro along with Symphony allows you to orchestrate any IT or Business operations with a Standard Operating Procedure such as querying incomplete sales order list, posting sales order, applying leaves, creating a ticket in ITSM, renewing SSL Certificate, monthly maintenance, executing patches, updates, upgrades, system copies, system refresh etc.
  • Automate: In the Production environment, Symphony works like an RPA automating workflows. It acts as a test management solution when run against QA environments, a job management solution when you schedule jobs, automating every job in your application portfolio.
  • Transform: Transform your business operations with Maestro, by auto-healing incidents through integration with monitoring solutions, enabling zero touch operations through integration with service management tools, running regression/non-functional/functional tests, governing user administration, and more.

Our winning entry at Cloud ALM Hack2Build hackathon covered:

  • Project Listing, Creating and Viewing Tasks in SAP Cloud ALM, viewing and managing tasks linked to specific projects, or adding new tasks to a project.
  • Real-Time Data Display of data fetched from SAP Cloud ALM. Maestro’s integration with Business Process Monitoring enables users to view live data, offering valuable insights.

 

Maestro – A Game Changer for Your Business

  • Seamless Process Automation across diverse systems, technologies, and applications 
  • Technologic-agnostic, future-proof, customizable workflows tailored to specific business needs
  • Efficiency and Productivity Gains through automation and comprehensive governance.

What to Expect in the Finals:

We’ll be demonstrating how Maestro can automate tasks across SAP CALM’s implementation, configuration, and operations. Maestro will show how it can slash administrators' task times from days to mere minutes.

 

Eviden

 

Eviden Cost Guard: Enhancing Cloud ALM with Financial Management

Eviden Cost Guard is a powerful extension to SAP’s Cloud ALM, designed to enhance project management by integrating financial oversight. It enables project managers and executives to track the financial health of projects and compare actual costs against baseline budgets. The tool offers real-time financial monitoring for both SAP and non-SAP projects.

Key Value:

  • Monitor and simulate project costs with ease
  • Analyse planned and actual costs
  • Compare costs against baseline budgets
  • Generate insights and recommendations for cost improvements

With Cost Guard, project managers can create a budget baseline and track changes, including those from change requests. The application allows for assigning cost rates to team members and accounting for additional expenses such as software, hardware, and travel. It provides real-time data integration from Cloud ALM, ensuring up-to-date cost tracking. Visual analytics allow users to analyse cost drivers and run "what-if" simulations to evaluate the impact of different cost distribution scenarios on the project’s financial performance.

Main features include a visual comparison of costs versus budgets, insights into cost drivers, and analytics to support cost optimization. With a user-friendly interface consistent with Cloud ALM, Cost Guard facilitates efficient, real-time project financial management.

 

Capgemini

Cloud ALM Mobile Device Workflow Approval

In today’s fast-paced business environment, managing Release and deployment Approvals can be challenging and time-consuming, especially when decision-makers are away from their desks. To optimize this process, it’s good to have the flexibility in approval process via Mobile Devices using Cloud ALM. This solution offers a user-friendly approval approach, making it easier to approve the changes with robust authentication and authorization mechanisms on mobile. One of the key elements of this Cloud ALM feature is the ability for users to monitor and manage tasks in real-time. To keep the process moving smoothly, the system sends email and push notification for defined approval steps directly to approver's mobile devices so that the approval can be done on time. SAP Cloud ALM Mobile Workflow Approval is an essential tool for modern businesses looking to optimize their release management processes.

In this case the trigger for the mobile workflow approval is located in the feature which is included in the release management in Cloud ALM. With this new feature the workflow step triggers the push notification and e-mail to the approver in real-time. The approval for the specific change/transport can be made via mobile device.

 

Oxando

Intelligent selective data transition to SAP Cloud ALM with oxando Data Migration

Problem Addressed:​
Customers have the need to identify the scope and amount of SAP Solution Manager data in particular processes and related documents relevant for the transition to SAP Cloud ALM. Selective data migration is essential for achieving a smooth transition to SAP Cloud ALM by selecting and moving only relevant data and ensuring the protection of investments done in process documentation.

Innovative Aspect:​
To reduce the time and effort for data migration we use AI Models for data selection and extraction, based on a process scope defined in SAP Cloud ALM. We fully automate the data import to SAP Cloud ALM.

How does the application work?

  • Define a target scope in SAP Cloud ALM based on the SAP Best Practices Content
  • Select the relevant structure in Solution Documentation and start the data retrieval process
  • AI Module will use a predefined (customizable) prompt to retrieve the data and return a proposal for the migration
  • Review and select the relevant data from the list
  • Transfer to SAP Cloud ALM target scope with a single click

 

Target audience:

Customers with large solution documentation

Partners who support customers with large solution documentation

entry.title: Re: OPSK field not editable in change mode in S/4 1709
entry.content: Note 3402593 will help fix the problem

entry.title: GDPR Sensitive BP, incoming payment proxy error
entry.content:
Hi,

environment: Hana FP2405. With GDPR enabled.

when trying to create incoming payment for a customer marked with sensitive data, service layer return Proxy Error.

Found Note 2715148, however it doesn’t seem to apply to this.

non sensitive BP no issue, only BP marked as sensitive.

please help.

Edy

entry.title: Material Analysis Report
entry.content:
MC.9 is a standard Material Analysis report by SAP.

The MC.9 report in SAP MM is a monthly stock analysis report that provides key figures for
materials, plants, and storage locations.

This is a Stock analysis report on Month basis.

Stock Analysis Report Path in SAP:

SAP Menu > Information Systems > Logistics > Inventory management > Material > MC.9
– Stock

T Code - MC.9

Enter Plant & Month which you want to get Stock Report as above shown & Execute.

Will get the stock report as above shown. But here we can get Material Description we need
to change the layout.

Click - Setting >Characteristics display > Key and Description

Thankyou.

entry.title: Re: GDPR Sensitive BP, incoming payment proxy error
entry.content:
Hello,

Hi,

environment: Hana FP2405. With GDPR enabled.   Official Site

when trying to create incoming payment for a customer marked with sensitive data, service layer return Proxy Error.

Found Note 2715148, however it doesn’t seem to apply to this.

non sensitive BP no issue, only BP marked as sensitive.

please help.

Edy

Best Regards

esther598

entry.title: How to use Action in a Task automation
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I'm new to Business Process Automation (BPA) and appreciate for any advice on a couple of issues I'm facing:

Background:
I'm working on a project that involves extracting data from Excel and using it to generate a Purchase Requisition Document via an API.

What I've Done So Far:
I've successfully used the necessary API to create and publish an Action (see Figure 1).

The Problems I'm Encountering:

  1. I'm trying to use this Action within a Task automation process, but I'm having trouble locating the option to invoke the Action in the right-hand panel of the screen (as shown in Figure 2). 

  2. Instead within the task automation, I can called a Process, and within that Process, I can invoke the Action. My question is, how do I set the input and output parameters for this Process? (Figure 3)

Any help you can offer would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Assigning the material name into CT04 characteristic
entry.content: That won't work since you need the class type 300 for the configuration - and as I understand you would like to use the material description for the configuration of the kMAT.

entry.title: Re: Extracting data from SAP’s spool job using BAPI_XBP_J...
entry.content:
@tyro You overlooked one essential step that is documented in the following document https://ftp.gwdg.de/pub/misc/sapdb/icc/bc-xbp/BCXBPTCV2V3.pdf

AUDITLEVEL should be raised from 0 to 2 to read spool content In my case it worked like a charm

It stated Page 35:

6.1 Reading the Content of a Spool Request Specified by Number Description In this scenario, the content of a spool request that has been specified by its number is to be retrieved via the XBP interface. Prerequisites A connection to an SAP system is established. The corrections of note 1171295 have to be in the test system. The audit level is set to the value 2, so that the use of the required functions can be verified in the XMI log (RZ15).

and Page 14

3.12 Setting the Audit Level XMI Description Set the audit level for the XMI logging higher/lower. This function is mandatory, because it is the only way to set the audit level within the XMI interface. Explanation: The XMI framework, which allows external Management Tools to log on to the SAP system and create XMI-sessions, also offers the possibility of writing a trace of the functions called during an XMI-session. The trace level can be set with the function BAPI_XMI_SET_AUDITLEVEL and can be viewed in the SAP system using transaction rz15. In the terminology of XMI, the trace level is called audit level. If the function BAPI_XMI_SET_AUDITLEVEL is not called explicitly in an XMI session, the audit level is 0 by default. Most XBP functions that make changes of some description in the system (create jobs, change jobs etc.), are already traced at audit level 0. Most XBP functions that only read information (such as BAPI_XBP_JOB_STATUS_GET) are only traced with audit level 2 or higher. Prerequisites A connection to an SAP R/3 System is established. A (XBP) session has to be established through the XMI interface. Realization The audit level of the XMI interface should be raised to 2 using the external job scheduling system. Use BAPI_XMI_SET_AUDITLEVEL . Then a status check of an existing job should be performed by the external scheduler If everything worked fine, set the audit level back to the default value 0.

entry.title: Re: Assigning the material name into CT04 characteristic
entry.content: Yes, exactly - I need the material name to be shown in the config profile so only class type 300 can be used there.

entry.title: Re: Is Manage Material Coverage a standalone app

entry.title: Re: Missing "yellow total line" and "Change layout button...

entry.title: How to separate automatic inventory accounts for goods issue using the same movement type (601)?
entry.content:
For each goods issue transaction, the accounting should be posted to different accounts. For example, sales goods issue is different from goods issue for free. However, in the current Best Practice Public Cloud, both of these transactions are assigned to the same Movement Type 601 (exp: BD9 vs BKA). Is there any way to change the movement type?

Thanks and best regards,

entry.title: Re: Eclipse, Field for creation user is missing in source...
entry.content: How did you do it exactly? I have replaced createdby and lastchangedby accordingly, but the error is still showing.

entry.title: Re: Currency conversion is not functioning incorrect setu...
entry.content: Could you check within Check Exchange Rate Types (102349 configuration activity) if there is still a kind of legacy 'reference currency' in your Rate Types Customizing after Change of Functional Currency execution ?

entry.title: Re: How to Consume SAP Data Warehouse Cloud in Power BI
entry.content:
Hi, 

Fantastic blog thanks for the insights! 

How can we secure the data exposed to Power BI? ODBC uses a database user, meaning DACs no longer apply. Therefore, in Datasphere should we maintain two sets of authorizations (DAC's at the DSP Level and Database User's at the Database level)? Or can you suggest an alternative approach? 

Thanks in advance for any advice you can provide on this issue.

entry.title: Re: Assigning the material name into CT04 characteristic
entry.content: In that case I have unfortunately no solution. Maybe someone else from the SAP community does. Otherwise, I would recommend you to look into programming a function that fetches the value from MAKT.

entry.title: How to completely refresh table control in dynpro
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have table control on dynpro screen 0300 - tc_301 table it_alc. 

I call screen 0300 from screen 0100, and Dynpro is filled with data. 

If there are more lines in table control, I scroll down. I close the screen 0300 with leave to screen 0 command, and I call screen 0300 again but dynpro shows the last position of scrolled line. How do I ensure that always the first line is at the top of the dynpro? 

Thank yo.

Also, I feel ideally it should be open SAC in the Excel itself and not in the external browser. Why is it doing this, any resolution?

But once I click on save, for the first time pop-up is being shown. But when I close the browser window or browser and then login to the same page settings are not persistent.

Could you please suggest what needs to be done or is it something related to patch upgrade.

Thanks,

Teja.

entry.title: Re: How to install DTW on client/workstation PC's with SA...
entry.content:
From the installation package of the version you install and that's it.
User, each one his own, so that the information of the user who created it is saved.

entry.title: Change Logs when using Fiori Applications
entry.content:
Hello experts,

Recently we have started to look at activating Standard Fiori applications for standard business transactions where users can Manage documents like Sales documents, Purchasing documents and Billing documents. We have noticed few things and want to understand how can this can be handled in a better way.

  • Origin of transaction missing when creating documents from Fiori. For example when we post a JV in finance from Fb01 we can see in the header of the accounting document the transaction is FB01 or FB70. But when we use Fiori to post this we see this empty. I can understand that BAPI is used for all updates but will be good to have either the App id or some kind of reference populated. this becomes a audit issue to track from where the document originates. The same goes for Change logs in Sales documents - Missing Tcode on the change logs. 
  • All the custom screen validation that was done in our PBO/PAI are not being called and again I assume its due to BAPI. Do we need to completely redesign all the validations or is there any other smarter approach? 
  • Attaching workflow into Standard Fiori apps - Sometimes we also see that business needs to have a approval flow for some master data records before being created. We can go to SAP build and do it or create custom Fiori app and enable workflow. Has anyone done it with using the standard Fiori application and attaching a workflow for approval and then once approved the master data getting created?  (this can be more a custom requirement) 
  • Another this is that the user is not having VA01 or VA02 access in the PFCG roles but when we activated Manage Sales order Version2 app and try to use the user got access to create the Sales order for the document type enabled via Fiori. We have only added the UI roles and not changes the existing role of the user. Again is this because of BAPI and how well can this be restricted using standard capabilities?

 

Appreciate your insights.

 

Br,

Vijay V

entry.title: Re: Db2 odbc connection to S4 HANA in RISE
entry.content: hi experts. Any suggestions please.

entry.title: Field for creation user is missing in source reference ZXX_GROCERY type TABL.
entry.content:
I'm trying to generate ABAP Repository Object by the instruction of "Building an ABAP RESTful Application" topic of Learning the Basics of ABAP Programming on SAP BTP provided by Learn SAP site.

But the generation of the repository object causes an error, displaying, "Field for creation user is missing in source reference ZXX_GROCERY type TABL." and "Field for change user is missing in source reference ZXX_GROCERY type TABL."

As per the solution that was given by SAP community, I modified the createdby field to abp_creation_user and lastchangedby to abp_lastchange_user, but the error still occurs. Please help.

entry.title: Re: 0FIAA_SIM values in 0FI_AA_12
entry.content:
Hi Karthik,

 

No, the 0FI_AA_12 extractor does not support generating simulated depreciation for future years beyond the current year. It typically provides data up to the current period, so it won't satisfy the requirement for future periods like 2025-2028.

entry.title: Re: How to continue executing batch job even after error
entry.content: I don't know RVV50R10C, so I don't know if it can run in background. I guess that it can send standard error messages and that will stop the background job. If you don't want to stop the job for your message, you should send it as type 'S' instead of 'E'. I guess that the user exit is called in other contexts, so you should add a condition.

entry.title: How the IP is logged in CAP audit log
entry.content: For CAP data access in audit log viewer I don't see IP address begin logged. How is IP added in the audit log?

entry.title: Re: Field for creation user is missing in source referenc...
entry.content:
Hi 

The error indicates that your table `ZXX_GROCERY` is missing required fields for user tracking (`CREATED_BY` and `LAST_CHANGED_BY`).

Here's how to fix it

1. Add Fields to Your Table
Open your table definition (`ZXX_GROCERY`).
Add two fields `CREATED_BY` and `LAST_CHANGED_BY`. Both should be of type `USER` or `CHAR` with length `12`.

2. Activate the Table
After adding the fields, activate the table.

3. Check Repository Object
Retry generating the repository object.

These fields are necessary for tracking creation and modification users in ABAP RESTful applications.

 

Regards,

Umasaral

entry.title: Re: Material Analysis Report
entry.content: Nice article. Unfortunately, MC.9 is no longer supported in S/4HANA ☹️

entry.title: Re: How to continue executing batch job even after error
entry.content:
Hello Sandra_Rossi,

RVV50R10C running through background job only but i can't make message as success because it will create the delivery for the current record, want to stop the execution for the current record when the validation is success and store the message in the job log and go for the next record to process. 

Will it work like this, if i write like below?

Inside the Implicit enhancement if i create a custom function module like below
call zfunc

exporting v1

exceptions

error_message = n_error

zfunc (implementation)

if xyz = '1'.

message ' Throw error' type 'E'.

endif.

entry.content:

hdbnsutil -sr_unregister needs a running HDB , must be done before stopping.

entry.title: Re: How to separate automatic inventory accounts for good...
entry.content:
Hello @laclac_1842 

Goods issue movement type is determined from schedule line category settings, transaction VOV6, e.g.

So to have different movement types, you need to customize different schedule line categories and their determination. Effectively, you end up with separate SD document flows for each transaction type, e.g. regular sales, free of charge delivery etc.

You can use a shortcut and modify movement types with custom enhancement in sales order processing.

I suppose the public cloud allows SD document flows configuration, not sure about custom enhancements though.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Production Execution Duration Fiori App Actual duration not reflrcting
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In SAP Release S4HANA 2022 FPS02, we are still not seeing data under Actual duration for majority of orders

There is difference between actual and planned duration. Any SAP note which is missing to be applied.

Regards

Nitin

entry.title: Re: Material Master Nomenclature
entry.content:
Hello @sagnikch 

As the legacy material number is 12 chars long, you can take it over directly to SAP as external material number. Material number in SAP is either 18 or 40 chars long, so the legacy numbers will fit nicely.

However bear in mind that external material numbering with any logic is not the best practice. Such logic will break sooner or later, business will come up with new requirements impacting the material numbering logic. However once a material is created, its number can't be changed. So there is no way to implement new requirements without recreating material master data. 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: BADI/exit to add new operations with IW31
entry.content: i have an  simliar Requirement , please update

entry.title: Re: Vendor Return process
entry.content: Thankyou.

entry.title: Re: Vendor Return process
entry.content: Yes.

entry.title: Re: integration options avaliable in abap
entry.content:
I think this question needs to be asked from the "opposite end": not "what integration technologies are available in ABAP" (because the answer to that would be dozens of pages long...), but "what kind of data does my non-sap system expect", and then based on that, you can select the most appropriate ABAP integration tool.

So perhaps you can provide more details about your non-sap system and what kind of data it needs/can process? Then I can perhaps give some recommendations into which SAP technology you should look in more detail (Web Service Engine, PI/PO/CPI, Business Connector, low level C/C++ / Java / C# library like RFC lib or JCo, IDoc or EDI interface, etc. etc.)

entry.title: Re: Tcode to see factory calendar like our Mobile calenda...
entry.content:
Hello @RaghavanKathamuthu 

You can get a very decent factory calendar overview in SCAL, e.g.

Is it not good enough?

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP MM
entry.content:
Hi

To write a Functional Specification Document (FSD) in SAP, follow these steps

1. Title Page
Title Functional Specification Document
Project Name
Document Number
Date
Author

2. Introduction
Purpose State the purpose of the document.
Scope Define the scope of the functionality to be developed.

3. Requirements
Business Requirements Describe the business need and objectives.
Functional Requirements Detail what the system should do, including inputs, outputs, and processing logic.

4. Process Flow
Flow Diagrams Include any process flow diagrams or data flow diagrams if needed.

5. Data Requirements
Data Elements Specify data fields, structures, and any data sources involved.

6. User Interface
Screens Describe the user interface, including mock-ups if possible.

7. Assumptions and Constraints
Assumptions Any assumptions made during the specification.
Constraints Constraints or limitations to be aware of.

8. Acceptance Criteria
Define the criteria that will be used to test the functionality.

9. Appendices
Include any additional information or references.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
HI, thanks for this post. I am already on unit 5 of Discovering End-to-End Business Processes for the Intelligent Enterprise, it seems that what I like the most is the SAP SuccessFactors solution I hope you can give me some guidance on where I should start, and what part you would recommend for SAP SF Consultant. 

 

My history: I am not from a tech background, I have been working in my country's Tax administration for over 15 years and we have been using SAP CRM and ERP since 2016. I am not a fan of finance taxes and accounting, so I want to change my profession completely.

entry.title: Re: SAP GRC Training and hands on experience
entry.content:
Hi @Gurpreet_Saroye,

Besides  the SAP Learning Hub subscription we offer a discounted student rate. 
Please access our student zone of SAP Learning website. there you can find the details. 
In addition we run digital skills initiative by SAP to boost your career with digital skills - Open new career opportunities with free learning and certification. 
Please check our Digital skills for SAP website to check if you are eligible to take part.

Kind regrds
Margit

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

I'm new to SAP and am excited about the opportunity to join the SAP Digital Skills Initiative. I come from an IT background with a focus on cloud computing and infrastructure management. With my experience in cloud services, as well as a strong foundation in technical support and customer service, I believe that expanding my skills in SAP technologies will greatly contribute to my professional growth.

I am particularly interested in the following certifications:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Provisioning and Administering Databases in SAP HANA Cloud
  • Installing and Administering SAP HANA

Thank you for consideration.

entry.title: Mass changes Equipment master in PM module
entry.content:
I need to make mass changes of Equipment master.
For example, to assign a different value of Cost centre to 1000 objects (Equipment master). How can I do it?
LSMW doesn't work and W is restricted in the context of data migrations to SAP S/4HANA (note 2287723)

Migration Cockpit is intended for initial data load - not for change. 
What should I do? Create ABAP-program for each case?

Thanks.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Mass changes Equipment master in PM module
entry.content: You can use the Mass Change feature in transaction IE05 to make changes to multiple equipments. Alternatively, for large volumes of data you could utilise BAPI_EQUI_CHANGE to develop a custom specific utility.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA for product Compliance NEW Dangerous Goods Ma...
entry.content: Hi Ajit. I have not performed the compliance assessment step for dangerous goods; this is the step that does not trigger. I am expecting for this assessment step to appear as soon as I have assigned the compliance purpose and roles as per the 3FC best practices test script.

entry.title: Re: RNIF adapter in CPI !
entry.content: Hi,It doesn't seem like SAP is interested in implementing an RNIF adapter in BTP. The decision on whether such an adapter will be developed has just been postponed to Q4/2025. We also urgently need such an adapter and will now likely have to look for alternatives.

entry.title: Re: RNIF adapter in CPI !
entry.content: Hi. It doesn't seem like SAP is interested in implementing an RNIF adapter in BTP. The decision on whether such an adapter will be developed has just been postponed to Q4/2025. We also urgently need such an adapter and will now likely have to look for alternatives.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
My name is Ahmed, and I'm thrilled to be part of the SAP Community Beginner Corner! I work in an organization focused on providing comprehensive SAP training and implementation services to businesses across the Middle East. Our mission is to empower companies to fully leverage the potential of SAP solutions.

I'm eager to dive into the SAP world and learn from the expertise within this community. I'm also keen on sharing my knowledge and experiences as I grow in my SAP journey.

Feel free to connect with me via private message. I'm excited to get started!

entry.title: Re: How to separate automatic inventory accounts for good...
entry.content:
Good afternoon @laclac_1842,

As of now, in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales, we can’t configure the schedule line categories. So we can't change the Movement type information. We need to use the standard configuration provided by SAP.

Regarding your requirement, I suggest you go through the link below. It might help you.

Setting Up Account Determination

Thank You

entry.title: Re: Sales BOM: Can we have components in the Sales BOM fr...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please check there is no option to choose plant at line item level.

Regards,

entry.title: Create Order window to pick Order Type based on Notification field value
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP standard functionality for Work order creation from Notification (IW21/IW22) is that once we click on the create button adjacent to Order field, the "Create Order" window pops-up. Based on Order-Notification Integration in SPRO, the Work Order type is proposed automatically along with additional data like default Work center and Planner Group. 

Now as per our client requirement we have 02 Order Types (PMA1 & PMA2)  for a single Notification type (Z1). We have 02 priorities (F1 & F2). Our requirement is that if F1 is selected then PMA1 appears in Create Order window or PMA2 when F2 priority is selected. 

Will appreciate inputs on this requirement.

Regards

Faisal

entry.title: Re: SAP GRC Training and hands on experience
entry.content:
Hello,

 

the Practice Systems are sold as a bundle, with the price you have access to all practice systems avaiable.

 

best regards

Jennifer Hauck

entry.title: Ariba CLID Integration to SAP S/4 Hana On-prem
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are working on the Ariba CLID Integration with SAP S/4 HANA on-prem. We have enabled the required parameters in ICM and able to see the Send to External System option in Ariba Contract workspace against the Line-item document. We have maintained the required master data in the External system integration and after we click on send to External system, we get a success message also in Contract Workspace.

However, we are not able to see this triggering any document in CIG or Business network, its like we clicked on Send to External system and nothing triggered on the system. Can some one please throw some light on what are we missing here? Once we click on Send to External system, where should we check the Contract is sent to S/4 Or not or how to trouble shoot?

NOTE: I went through the standard links in help.sap.com, but there were not helpful, any help on this would be very much appreciated.

 

Thanks,
Velu

entry.title: Re: Sales BOM: Is it possible to have Sales BOM that coul...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

It is possible enter service and material items.

 

For the material choose Item category as L and for the service choose item category as N.

 

Hope it is helpful if any please revert.

 

Regards,

entry.title: Re: In SAC, using the Data Export API, how do I export the dimension hierarchies?
entry.content: From what I can tell, it's not possible.

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting SAP Certification discount code for special programs
entry.content:
Mr. David, I beg you to help me solve the problem of the campaign not accepting the activation code.

I have followed all the instructions and steps that you sent to me but it still doesn't want to accept redeem.

@David_Chaviano

entry.title: Re: How to connect to SAP HANA DB using X.509 certificate in hdbuserstore
entry.content:
How does this work for secondary DB connection?

Lets say my main ERP system is connected to DB via x509 authentication and now I also need to establish secondary DB connection to another SAP system (via dbacockpit).

entry.title: Re: In SAC, using the Data Export API, how do I export th...
entry.content:
Looking at the blog

SAP Analytics Cloud Export Data API Sample Scripts - SAP Community

Q2: When retrieving a dimension the column 'parent' (that is used by SAP Analytics Cloud for hierarchical data structures) is not returned, why is this?

A2: The hierarchy is part of an internal hierarchy extension and doesn't form part of the data retrieval and they don't form part of the API. There are no plans to change this.

 

One of the workaround is that we'll just have to create new dimension members and manually copy the hierarchy values into there so it becomes data and can be exported.  It's a bit of a pain.
